steps.title: Test ordered pair (1, 1)
steps.content:
Substitute \(x = 1\) and \(y = 1\) into the inequality 5x -3y < 4:
$$5(1) -3(1) = 2 < 4.$$ This is true, so (1, 1) is a solution.

steps.title: Test ordered pair (2, 5)
steps.content:
Next, substitute \(x = 2\) and \(y = 5\):
$$5(2) -3(5) = 10 - 15 = -5 < 4.$$ This is true as well, so (2, 5) also satisfies the inequality.

steps.title: Test ordered pair (3, 2)
steps.content:
Finally, substitute \(x = 3\) and \(y = 2\):
$$5(3) -3(2) = 15 - 6 = 9 < 4.$$ This is false, since 9 is not less than 4.

steps.title: Conclude the correct option
steps.content:
From tests above, only ordered pairs (1, 1) and (2, 5) satisfy the inequality.
Therefore, choice C is correct.
